question: Find compounds whose name ends with 'one' that are used to treat the 'hypertension'. What genes do they downregulate?
cypher: MATCH (c1:Compound)-[:TREATS_CtD]->(d:Disease {name: 'hypertension'}) MATCH (g1:Gene)<-[:DOWNREGULATES_CdG]-(c1) WHERE c1.name ENDS WITH 'one' RETURN c1.name AS Compound1, g1.name AS Gene1

question: Which genes on chromosome 'X' that are covaried with genes participate in the pathway named 'Apoptosis'?
List cellular components whose name contains 'collagene' in which the genes which are either upregulated in the 'ear' or expressed in the 'sense organ' participate.
cypher: MATCH (cc:CellularComponent)<-[:PARTICIPATES_GpCC]-(g:Gene) WHERE (g)<-[:UPREGULATES_AuG]-(:Anatomy {name: 'ear'}) OR (g)<-[:EXPRESSES_AeG]-(:Anatomy {name: 'sense organ'}) AND cc.name CONTAINS 'collagen' RETURN g.name, cc.name

question: Which genes whose name starts with 'HIST' participate in the cellular component with identifier 'GO:0000784' or with name 'histone deacetylase complex'?
cypher: MATCH (g:Gene)-[:PARTICIPATES_GpCC]->(cc:CellularComponent) WHERE (cc.identifier = 'GO:0000784' OR cc.name = 'histone deacetylase complex') AND g.name STARTS WITH 'HIST' RETURN g.name

question: What are the distinct anatomical locations whose name include 'system' associated with genes in cellular component with identifier 'GO:0034677' or with name 'nuclear chromosome, telomeric region'?
cypher: MATCH (a:Anatomy)-[b:EXPRESSES_AeG]->(g:Gene)-[:PARTICIPATES_GpCC]->(cc:CellularComponent)  WHERE (cc.name = 'nuclear chromosome, telomeric region' OR cc.identifier = 'GO:0034677') AND a.name CONTAINS 'system' RETURN DISTINCT a.name

question: Which five genes participate in the fewest cellular components (breaking ties alphabetically according to gene name)?
cypher: MATCH (g:Gene)-[r:PARTICIPATES_GpCC]->(cc:CellularComponent) WITH g, COUNT(r) AS participationCount RETURN g.name, participationCount ORDER BY participationCount ASC, g.name ASC LIMIT 5

question: Which distinct genes participate in both cellular components and pathways as the gene 'HIST1H4F' and are located on chromosome '1'?
cypher: MATCH (g1:Gene {name: 'HIST1H4F'})-[:PARTICIPATES_GpCC]->(cc:CellularComponent), (g1)-[:PARTICIPATES_GpPW]->(pw:Pathway), (g2:Gene)-[:PARTICIPATES_GpCC]->(cc) WHERE (g2)-[:PARTICIPATES_GpPW]->(pw) AND g2.chromosome = '1' RETURN DISTINCT g2.name

question: Retrieve diseases that are similar to 'obesity' and have associated genes located on chromosome 9.
cypher: MATCH (d1:Disease {name: 'obesity'})-[:RESEMBLES_DrD]->(d2:Disease)-[:ASSOCIATES_DaG]->(g:Gene) WHERE g.chromosome = '9' RETURN d2.name, g.name

question: Retrieve genes whose names contain 'USP', such that they interact with a gene that participates in both the 'Cell Cycle' and 'DNA Repair' pathways.
cypher: MATCH (g:Gene)-[:PARTICIPATES_GpPW]->(p1:Pathway {name: 'Cell Cycle'}), (g)-[:PARTICIPATES_GpPW]->(p2:Pathway {name: 'DNA Repair'}), (g)-[:INTERACTS_GiG]->(g1:Gene) WHERE g1.name CONTAINS'USP' RETURN g1.name

question: Which distinct molecular functions related to 'RNA' do genes that participate in the biological process 'regulation of heart contraction' also participate in?
cypher: MATCH (bp:BiologicalProcess {name: 'regulation of heart contraction'})<-[:PARTICIPATES_GpBP]-(g:Gene)-[:PARTICIPATES_GpMF]->(mf:MolecularFunction) mf.name CONTAINS 'RNA' RETURN DISTINCT mf.name

question: What distinct GTPase-related molecular functions do genes participating in the biological process 'cytokinesis' or 'chemotaxis' participate in
cypher: MATCH (g:Gene)-[:PARTICIPATES_GpBP]->(bp:BiologicalProcess), (g)-[:PARTICIPATES_GpMF]->(mf:MolecularFunction) WHERE (bp.name = 'cytokinesis' OR bp.name = 'chemotaxis') AND mf.name CONTAINS 'GTPase' RETURN DISTINCT mf.name

question: Which biological process has the fewest participating genes, breaking ties alphabetically by biological process name?
cypher: MATCH (g:Gene)-[:PARTICIPATES_GpBP]->(bp:BiologicalProcess) RETURN bp.name, COUNT(g) AS gene_count ORDER BY gene_count ASC, bp.name ASC LIMIT 1

question: Which pharmacologic class has the minimum number of compounds (breaking ties alphabetically according to pharmacologic class name), and what is the count?
cypher: MATCH (p:PharmacologicClass)-[:INCLUDES_PCiC]->(c:Compound) RETURN p.name, COUNT(c) AS compound_count ORDER BY compound_count ASC, p.name ASC LIMIT 1

question: List symptoms whose name contains 'Pain'. Which are associated with diseases that localize to 'liver'?
cypher: MATCH (a:Anatomy {name: 'liver'})<-[:LOCALIZES_DlA]-(d:Disease)-[:PRESENTS_DpS]->(s:Symptom) WHERE s.name CONTAINS 'Pain' RETURN s.name AS symptom_name

question: List genes on chromosome 'Y' that are co-expressed in the lung and sense organ.
cypher: MATCH (a1:Anatomy {name: 'sense organ'})-[:EXPRESSES_AeG]->(g:Gene) MATCH (a2:Anatomy {name: 'lung'})-[:EXPRESSES_AeG]->(g) WHERE g.chromosome = 'Y' RETURN g.name AS GeneName

question: List distinct RNA-related pathways in which the genes that interact with gene 'DFFA' participate.
cypher: MATCH (g:Gene)-[:INTERACTS_GiG]->(g1:Gene)-[:PARTICIPATES_GpPW]->(p:Pathway) WHERE g.name = 'DFFA' AND p.name CONTAINS 'RNA' RETURN DISTINCT g1.name,p.name

question: Retrieve distinct blood-related anatomical locations that diseases presenting the symptom 'Fever' localize to.
cypher: MATCH (s:Symptom {name: 'Fever'})<-[:PRESENTS_DpS]-(d:Disease)-[:LOCALIZES_DlA]->(a:Anatomy) WHERE a.name CONTAINS 'blood' RETURN DISTINCT a.name

question: Which five genes are expressed in the highest number of distinct anatomical locations (breaking ties alphabetically according to gene name)?
cypher: MATCH (a:Anatomy)-[:EXPRESSES_AeG]->(g:Gene) RETURN g.name, COUNT(DISTINCT a) AS expression_count ORDER BY expression_count DESC, g.name ASC LIMIT 5

question: Look for compunds whose name ends with 'nin' and which downregulate the genes participating the biological process 'endocytosis' or 'viral process'.
cypher: MATCH (c:Compound)-[:DOWNREGULATES_CdG]->(g:Gene)-[:PARTICIPATES_GpBP]->(bp:BiologicalProcess) WHERE c.name ENDS WITH 'nin' AND bp.name in ['endocytosis', 'viral process'] RETURN DISTINCT c.name

question: Which disease associated with genes is linked to the highest number of pathways (breaking ties alphabetically according to disease name), and what is that number?
cypher: MATCH (d:Disease)-[:ASSOCIATES_DaG]->(g:Gene)-[:PARTICIPATES_GpPW]->(p:Pathway) WITH d, COUNT(DISTINCT p) AS pathway_count RETURN d.name, pathway_count ORDER BY pathway_count DESC, d.name ASC LIMIT 1

question: Find unique diseases upregulating or downregulating the genes which participate in the pathway 'Oxidative phosphorylation'.
Retrieve two genes both in the same chromosome ('1') interacting with each other and both of them participate in the pathway 'Vpu mediated degradation of CD4'.
cypher: MATCH (g1:Gene)-[:PARTICIPATES_GpPW]->(pw:Pathway {name: 'Vpu mediated degradation of CD4'})<-[:PARTICIPATES_GpPW]-(g2:Gene) WHERE (g1)-[:INTERACTS_GiG]->(g2) AND g1.chromosome = g2.chromosome AND g1.chromosome = '1' RETURN g1.name, g2.name

question: What are the compounds whose name start with 'P' that upregulate or downregulate gene whose identifier is 51026?
cypher: MATCH (c1:Compound)-[r1:UPREGULATES_CuG]->(g:Gene{identifier:51026})<-[r2:DOWNREGULATES_CdG]-(c2:Compound) WHERE c1.name STARTS WITH 'P' AND c2.name STARTS WITH 'P' RETURN c1.name, c2.name

question: Find genes that are regulated by the gene whose identifier is 83448 and the compounds by which they are bound with actions including 'activator'.
cypher: MATCH (g1:Gene{identifier:83448})-[:REGULATES_GrG]->(g2:Gene)<-[r:BINDS_CbG]-(c:Compound) WHERE 'activator' IN r.actions RETURN g1.name, g2.name, c.name, r.actions

question: What are the molecular functions that the genes downregulated by compound 'Nicergoline' participate in?
Retrieve the names of compounds starting wth the letter 'B' which bind the gene whose name is 'CYP1A2' and where the binding action includes 'inhibitor' or 'substrate'.
cypher: MATCH (c:Compound)-[r:BINDS_CbG]->(g:Gene {name: 'CYP1A2'}) WHERE 'inhibitor' IN r.actions OR 'substrate' IN r.actions AND c.name STARTS WITH 'B' RETURN c.name, r.actions

question: List diseases that resemble the disease whose identifier is 'DOID:1793'. Skip first two diseases.
Identify the cellular components associated with the genes named 'TP53' and 'BRCA1'.
cypher: MATCH (g:Gene)-[:PARTICIPATES_GpCC]->(cc:CellularComponent) WHERE g.name = 'TP53' OR g.name = 'BRCA1' RETURN cc.name

question: Which genes do not interact with other genes on chromosome 'X'? Return the gene name.
cypher: MATCH (g:Gene) WHERE NOT (g)-[:INTERACTS_GiG]->(:Gene {chromosome: 'X'}) RETURN g.name AS gene

question: List genes participating in the biological process 'female sex differentiation' that are associated with 'azoospermia'.
Find all genes that are downregulated in 'systemic scleroderma'.
Retrieve genes associated with either 'diabetes mellitus' or 'hypertension'
cypher: MATCH (g:Gene)<-[:ASSOCIATES_DaG]-(d:Disease) WHERE d.name = 'diabetes mellitus' OR d.name = 'hypertension' RETURN g.name

question: Find diseases associated with genes that are participating either in the 'Wnt signaling pathway' or in the 'Notch signaling pathway'.
cypher: MATCH (d:Disease)-[:ASSOCIATES_DaG]->(g:Gene)-[:PARTICIPATES_GpPW]->(pw:Pathway) WHERE pw.name = 'Wnt signaling pathway' OR pw.name = 'Notch signaling pathway' RETURN d.name

question: List all distinct compounds whose name ends with 'en' that either upregulate or downregulate genes associated with the 'obesity' dosease.
cypher: MATCH (c:Compound)-[:UPREGULATES_CuG|:DOWNREGULATES_CdG]->(g:Gene)<-[:ASSOCIATES_DaG]-(d:Disease {name: 'obesity'}) WHERE c.name ENDS WITH 'en' RETURN DISTINCT c.name

question: Which genes are expressed in the 'brain' and either upregulated or downregulated in 'Schizophrenia'?
What distinct pathways do genes that interact with gene 'BRCA1' participate in?
Which genes participate in both the 'DNA Repair' and 'Cell Cycle' pathways?
Identify genes that interact with 'MYC' and participate in either 'Cell Cycle' or 'Wnt Signaling' pathways.
cypher: MATCH (g:Gene)-[:INTERACTS_GiG]->(:Gene {name: 'MYC'})-[:PARTICIPATES_GpPW]->(p:Pathway) WHERE p.name = 'Cell Cycle' OR p.name = 'Wnt Signaling' RETURN g.name

question: Find pathways that the gene 'BRCA1' and the gene with identifier '8357' both participate in.
Identify compounds that upregulate genes involved in the molecular function 'syntaxin binding'.
Which biological processes do genes participating in the molecular function 'U1 snRNA binding' participate?
List the molecular functions associated with genes that participate in the biological process 'protein sialylation', ordered by the number of participating genes in descending order.
cypher: MATCH (bp:BiologicalProcess {name: 'protein sialylation'})<-[:PARTICIPATES_GpBP]-(g:Gene)-[:PARTICIPATES_GpMF]->(mf:MolecularFunction) RETURN mf.name, COUNT(g) AS gene_count ORDER BY gene_count DESC

question: What is the average number of biological processes in which a gene participating in the molecular function 'kinase activity' participate?
cypher: MATCH (mf:MolecularFunction {name: 'kinase activity'})<-[:PARTICIPATES_GpMF]-(g:Gene)-[:PARTICIPATES_GpBP]->(bp:BiologicalProcess) WITH g, COUNT(bp) AS process_count RETURN AVG(process_count) AS avg_processes_per_gene

question: What are the distinct side effects whose name mentions 'Acute' caused by compounds that treat 'breast cancer' disease?
cypher: MATCH (d:Disease {name: 'breast cancer'})<-[:TREATS_CtD]-(c:Compound)-[:CAUSES_CcSE]->(se:SideEffect) WHERE se.name CONTAINS 'Acute' RETURN DISTINCT se.name

question: Which genes are upregulated by compounds that treat 'melanoma'?
Which anatomical locations are affected by diseases treated by the compound 'Linagliptin'?
Which symptoms are presented by diseases treated by the compound 'Mesalazine'?
What are the pharmacologic classes that include compounds that treat 'migraine'?
List compounds that treat diseases localized to either the cardiovascular or respiratory system.
cypher: MATCH (c:Compound)-[:TREATS_CtD]->(d:Disease)-[:LOCALIZES_DlA]->(a:Anatomy) WHERE a.name = 'cardiovascular system' OR a.name = 'respiratory system' RETURN c.name

question: Which distinct compounds treating diseases present symptoms related to 'Pain'?
What side effects are caused by compounds included in the pharmacologic class with the identifier 'N0000007151'?
Which genes are downregulated by compounds included in the pharmacologic class 'Dopamine Uptake Inhibitors'?
List the pharmacologic classes including compounds that treat the disease 'glaucoma', sorted by the pharmacologic class name.
cypher: MATCH (p:PharmacologicClass)-[:INCLUDES_PCiC]->(c:Compound)-[:TREATS_CtD]->(d:Disease {name: 'glaucoma'}) RETURN p.name, c.name ORDER BY p.name

question: Look for the average number of diseases treated by compounds included in each pharmacologic class.
cypher: MATCH (p:PharmacologicClass)-[:INCLUDES_PCiC]->(c:Compound)-[:TREATS_CtD]->(d:Disease) WITH p, COUNT(d) AS disease_count RETURN AVG(disease_count) AS average_disease_count

question: Identify diseases that present multiple symptoms including 'Headache' and 'Nausea'.
Which symptoms are presented by the disease identified by 'DOID:0060119' or 'DOID:1686'?
cypher: MATCH (d:Disease)-[:PRESENTS_DpS]->(s:Symptom) WHERE d.identifier = 'DOID:0060119' OR d.identifier = 'DOID:1686' RETURN s.name AS symptom_name

question: Find the average number of diseases that localize to an anatomical part.
cypher: MATCH (d:Disease)-[:LOCALIZES_DlA]->(a:Anatomy) WITH a, COUNT(d) AS disease_count RETURN AVG(disease_count) AS avg_diseases_per_anatomy

question: Which genes are expressed in the vagina and downregulated in 'lung cancer'?
Which genes are expressed in the heart and participate in the regulation of Biological Processes 'reproductive system development'?
Which pairs of distinct genes on chromosome 2 both participate in the same pathway and are both associated with the molecular function 'frizzled binding'? Return the two gene names.
cypher: MATCH (g1:Gene)-[:PARTICIPATES_GpPW]->(pw:Pathway) MATCH (g1)-[:PARTICIPATES_GpMF]->(mf:MolecularFunction {name: 'frizzled binding'}) MATCH (g2:Gene)-[:PARTICIPATES_GpPW]->(pw) WHERE (mf)<-[:PARTICIPATES_GpMF]-(g2) AND g2.name <> g1.name AND g1.chromosome = g2.chromosome = '2' RETURN g1.name AS GeneName1, g2.name AS GeneName2

question: What is the average number of genes expressed in the anatomical structure?
cypher: MATCH (a:Anatomy)-[:EXPRESSES_AeG]->(g:Gene) WITH a, COUNT(g) as gene_count RETURN AVG(gene_count)

question: Which genes covary with the gene 'MYBPC2' or gene 'LIMS1'?
cypher: MATCH (g:Gene)-[:COVARIES_GcG]->(covarying:Gene) WHERE g.name = 'MYBPC2' OR g.name = 'LIMS1' RETURN covarying.name

question: What is the average number of genes regulated by each gene?
cypher: MATCH (g:Gene)-[:REGULATES_GrG]->(regulated:Gene) WITH g, COUNT(regulated) as gene_count RETURN AVG(gene_count)

question: What are the side effects caused by compounds that upregulate the gene 'GPATCH8' and are included in the pharmacologic class 'Thyroxine'?
Retrieve the anatomical locations that are localized in diseases presenting the symptom 'Obesity' and that resemble 'atherosclerosis'.
If a patient is taking the 'Gemcitabine' to treat his disease, what distinct symptoms he may present before the treatment?
What are the compounds and diseases that the compounds treat, knowing that the compounds cause 'Vertigo' and that the diseases present 'Headache'?
What are the compounds that a patient with 'ovarian cancer' takes and that cause 'Abdominal distension'?
cypher: MATCH (se:SideEffect)<-[:CAUSES_CcSE]-(c:Compound)-[:TREATS_CtD]->(d:Disease) WHERE se.name = 'Abdominal distension' AND d.name = 'ovarian cancer' RETURN c.name

question: Which diseases present the 'Paraplegia'? Find the number of compounds which treat each kind of these diseases.
What are the compounds and the diseases that they treat, knowing that both the side effects caused by compounds and the symptoms presented by diseases are 'Vomiting'?
Which genes participate in the biological process 'cell-cell signaling' and are bound by the compound 'Triazolam'?
Which genes participate in the biological process 'sexual reproduction' are downregulated by compounds included in the pharmacologic class 'Thiazolidinediones'?
What are the unique common biological processes shared by genes bound by compounds included in both the pharmacologic classes 'Decreased Platelet Aggregation' and 'Thyroxine' ?
List the distinct biological processes in which the genes bound by 'Isoaminile' or 'Methocarbamol' participate.
cypher: MATCH (c:Compound)-[:BINDS_CbG]->(g:Gene)-[:PARTICIPATES_GpBP]->(bp:BiologicalProcess) WHERE c.name IN ['Isoaminile', 'Methocarbamol'] RETURN DISTINCT bp.name

question: Which genes participate in the pathway 'Metabolism' and are upregulated by the disease 'azoospermia'?
What diseases downregulate the genes which paticipate in the pathway 'Integration of provirus'?
Look for the distinct diseases that associate the genes which participate in the pathways 'Signaling by GPCR' and 'Oxidative Stress'.
cypher: MATCH (pw1:Pathway {name: 'Signaling by GPCR'})<-[:PARTICIPATES_GpPW]-(:Gene)<-[:ASSOCIATES_DaG]-(d1:Disease)-[:ASSOCIATES_DaG]->(:Gene)-[:PARTICIPATES_GpPW]->(pw2:Pathway {name: 'Oxidative Stress'}) RETURN DISTINCT d1.name

question: Which distinct diseases resemble Behcet-related disease' and downregulate the genes which participate in the pathway 'Late Phase of HIV Life Cycle'?
cypher: MATCH (d1:Disease)-[:RESEMBLES_DrD]->(d2:Disease)-[:DOWNREGULATES_DdG]->(g:Gene)-[:PARTICIPATES_GpPW]->(pw:Pathway {name: 'Late Phase of HIV Life Cycle'}) WHERE d1.name CONTAINS 'Behcet' RETURN DISTINCT d2.name

question: What is the average number of pathways that genes downregulated by the disease 'obesity' are associated with?
cypher: MATCH (d:Disease {name: 'obesity'})-[:DOWNREGULATES_DdG]->(g:Gene)-[:PARTICIPATES_GpPW]->(p:Pathway) WITH g, COUNT(p) AS pathway_count RETURN AVG(pathway_count)

question: What are the diseases, whose name does not contain 'cancer', treated by the compound whose identifier is 'DB00997'?
cypher: MATCH (n:Compound {identifier: 'DB00997'})-[r:TREATS_CtD]->(d:Disease) WHERE NOT d.name CONTAINS 'cancer' RETURN n.name, d.name

question: Identify diseases whose name contains 'chronic' where the associated genes are bound by Tacrolimus.
cypher: MATCH (c:Compound {name: 'Tacrolimus'})-[r1:BINDS_CbG]->(g:Gene)<-[:ASSOCIATES_DaG]-(d:Disease) WHERE d.name CONTAINS 'chronic' RETURN g.name, d.name

question: List the names of compounds that bind to gene whose identifier is 3362 and also the diseases name that they palliate contain the word 'Tourette'.
cypher: MATCH (c:Compound)-[r1:BINDS_CbG]->(g:Gene {identifier: 3362}), (c)-[:PALLIATES_CpD]->(d:Disease) WHERE d.name CONTAINS 'Tourette' RETURN g.name, c.name, d.name

question: What are the diseases whose associated genes starting with the letter 'A' bind the compound 'Progesterone' and have a binding affinity between 10 and 100 nM ?
cypher: MATCH (d:Disease)-[:ASSOCIATES_DaG]->(g:Gene)<-[r:BINDS_CbG]-(c:Compound {name: 'Progesterone'}) WHERE r.affinity_nM >= 10 AND r.affinity_nM <= 100 AND g.name STARTS WITH 'A' RETURN g.name, r.affinity_nM, d.name

question: Retrieve the names of compounds starting wth the letter 'B' which bind the gene whose name is 'CYP1A2' and where the binding action includes 'inhibitor' or 'substrate'.
cypher: MATCH (c:Compound)-[r:BINDS_CbG]->(g:Gene {name: 'CYP1A2'}) WHERE 'inhibitor' IN r.actions OR 'substrate' IN r.actions AND c.name STARTS WITH 'B' RETURN c.name, r.actions

question: List diseases that resemble the disease whose identifier is 'DOID:1793'. Skip first two diseases.
Which malignant-related diseases present the similar symptom with the disease 'hypertension'? List the distinct diseases names.
cypher: MATCH (d1:Disease {name: 'hypertension'})-[:PRESENTS_DpS]->(s:Symptom)<-[:PRESENTS_DpS]-(d2:Disease) WHERE d2.name CONTAINS 'malignant' RETURN DISTINCT d2.name AS Disease2

question: What are the compounds that resemble the compound with identifier 'DB01603' and that have a similarity score greater than 0.7? Start listing the compounds by the largest similarity.
cypher: MATCH (c1:Compound {identifier: 'DB01603'})-[r:RESEMBLES_CrC]->(c2:Compound) WHERE r.similarity > 0.7 RETURN c1.name, c2.name, r.similarity ORDER BY r.similarity DESC

question: Find the number of compounds that cause the same side effects as 'Alitretinoin' and also downregulate the same genes.
How many genes participate in the cellular component with name 'chromosomal region' or 'excitatory synapse'?
cypher: MATCH (g:Gene)-[:PARTICIPATES_GpCC]->(cc:CellularComponent) WHERE cc.name = 'chromosomal region' or cc.name = 'excitatory synapse' RETURN COUNT(g)

question: How many genes are associated with the cellular component 'TAP complex'?
Which cellular components have at least 500 participating genes, ranked from highest to lowest by gene count?
cypher: MATCH (g:Gene)-[:PARTICIPATES_GpCC]->(cc:CellularComponent) WITH cc, COUNT(g) AS geneCount WHERE geneCount >= 500 RETURN cc.name, geneCount ORDER BY geneCount DESC

question: Which genes are regulated by 'NPC1' and associated with 'azoospermia'?
How many genes are associated with the disease 'salivary gland cancer'?
How many genes are involved in either the 'Apoptosis' pathway or the 'Cell Cycle' pathway?
cypher: MATCH (g:Gene)-[:PARTICIPATES_GpPW]->(p:Pathway) WHERE p.name = 'Apoptosis' OR p.name = 'Cell Cycle' RETURN COUNT(g)

question: How many pathways does the gene 'TP53' participate in?
Which pathways have at least 500 participating genes, ranked from highest to lowest by gene count?
cypher: MATCH (g:Gene)-[:PARTICIPATES_GpPW]->(p:Pathway) WITH p, COUNT(g) AS geneCount WHERE geneCount >= 500 RETURN p.name, geneCount ORDER BY geneCount DESC

question: List the RNA-related pathways by the number of genes on chromosome 'X' that they involve. Start with the pathway that involves the most genes.
cypher: MATCH (g:Gene)-[:PARTICIPATES_GpPW]->(p:Pathway) WHERE g.chromosome = 'X' AND p.name CONTAINS 'RNA' RETURN p.name, COUNT(g) AS gene_count ORDER BY gene_count DESC

question: Which gene covatirs with the gene 'PIK3CA' and participates in the pathway 'TOR Signaling'?
Which gene participates in the 'DNA metabolic process' and 'U1 snRNA binding'?
cypher: MATCH (mf:MolecularFunction {name: 'U1 snRNA binding'})<-[:PARTICIPATES_GpMF]-(g:Gene)-[:PARTICIPATES_GpBP]->(bp:BiologicalProcess {name: 'DNA metabolic process'}) RETURN g.name

question: How many distinct molecular functions are associated with genes involved in 'regulation of heart contraction'?
cypher: MATCH (bp:BiologicalProcess {name: 'regulation of heart contraction'})<-[:PARTICIPATES_GpBP]-(g:Gene)-[:PARTICIPATES_GpMF]->(mf:MolecularFunction) RETURN COUNT(DISTINCT mf) AS distinct_molecular_functions

question: How many different genes participate in both 'establishment of protein localization to chromatin' and 'postsynaptic membrane assembly'?
cypher: MATCH (g:Gene)-[:PARTICIPATES_GpBP]->(bp:BiologicalProcess) WHERE bp.name = 'establishment of protein localization to chromatin' OR bp.name = 'postsynaptic membrane assembly' RETURN COUNT(DISTINCT g) AS gene_count

question: Find the number of side effects caused by compounds treating hypertension.
cypher: MATCH (d:Disease {name: 'hypertension'})<-[:TREATS_CtD]-(c:Compound)-[:CAUSES_CcSE]->(se:SideEffect) RETURN COUNT(Distinct se) AS side_effect_count

question: Which compounds that treat multiple sclerosis cause the fewest side effects? Return the top 3, breaking ties alphabetically by compound name.
cypher: MATCH (d:Disease {name: 'multiple sclerosis'})<-[:TREATS_CtD]-(c:Compound)-[:CAUSES_CcSE]->(se:SideEffect) WITH c, COUNT(se) AS side_effect_count RETURN c.name, side_effect_count ORDER BY side_effect_count ASC, c.name ASC LIMIT 3

question: How many pharmacologic classes include compounds that palliate 'breast cancer'?
cypher: MATCH (pc:PharmacologicClass)-[:INCLUDES_PCiC]->(c:Compound)-[:PALLIATES_CpD]->(d:Disease {name: 'breast cancer'}) RETURN COUNT(pc) AS pharmacologic_class_count

question: Count the number of genes that are downregulated by compounds treating nicotine dependence.
Find the compounds that bind to the gene with name 'CYP3A4' and belong to the pharmacologic class 'Central Nervous System Stimulation'.
Which compounds included in the pharmacologic class 'Benzothiazoles' cause the side effect identified by 'C0030193'?
What is the average number of compounds included in each pharmacologic class?
cypher: MATCH (p:PharmacologicClass)-[:INCLUDES_PCiC]->(c:Compound) WITH p, COUNT(c) AS compound_count RETURN AVG(compound_count) AS average_compound_count

question: Which diseases localizing in 'retina' present with the symptom 'Obesity'?
What is the maximum number of diseases that localize to any single anatomical part?
cypher: MATCH (d:Disease)-[:LOCALIZES_DlA]->(a:Anatomy) WITH a, COUNT(d) AS disease_count RETURN MAX(disease_count) AS max_disease_count

question: List the diseases that localize to 'gonad', ordered by the highest number of presented symptoms.
cypher: MATCH (a:Anatomy {name: 'gonad'})<-[:LOCALIZES_DlA]-(d:Disease)-[:PRESENTS_DpS]->(s:Symptom) RETURN d.name AS disease_name, COUNT(s) AS symptom_count

question: What is the total number of symptoms presented by diseases that localize to 'decidua'?
What is the average number of symptoms presented by diseases localizing to 'heart'?
cypher: MATCH (:Anatomy {name: 'heart'})<-[:LOCALIZES_DlA]-(d:Disease)-[:PRESENTS_DpS]->(s:Symptom) WITH d, COUNT(s) AS symptom_count RETURN AVG(symptom_count) AS avg_symptoms

question: What is the maximum number of genes expressed in a single anatomical structure?
cypher: MATCH (a:Anatomy)-[:EXPRESSES_AeG]->(g:Gene) WITH a,COUNT(g) AS gene_count RETURN MAX(gene_count)

question: Find the maximum number of genes which are downregulated by a anatomical structure and upregulated by 'bone marrow'.
cypher: MATCH (a:Anatomy)-[:DOWNREGULATES_AdG]->(g:Gene)<-[:UPREGULATES_AuG]-(b:Anatomy {name: 'bone marrow'}) WITH a,COUNT(g) as gene_count RETURN MAX(gene_count)

question: Order the bone-related anatomical structures by the number of diseases which localize in each of them. Start with the largest number.
cypher: MATCH (d:Disease)-[:LOCALIZES_DlA]->(a:Anatomy) WITH a, COUNT(d) AS disease_count WHERE a.name CONTAINS 'bone' RETURN a.name, disease_count ORDER BY disease_count DESC

question: Which genes on chromosome 'Y' participate in the highest number of pathways, ranked from highest to lowest by pathway count?

question: List the genes that are bound by compounds causing the side effect 'Nausea' and binding 'affinity_nM' equal to 1000.

With which genes does the gene 'TP53' interact, when the interaction is supported by more that 3 sources including 'hetio-dag'?
cypher: MATCH (g1:Gene {name: 'TP53'})-[r:INTERACTS_GiG]->(g2:Gene) WHERE 'hetio-dag' IN r.sources AND SIZE(r.sources) > 3 RETURN g2.name, r.sources

question: Can you list genes that participate in the 'germ cell nucleus' cellular component?
Which compounds are included in the 'Antibiotics' pharmacologic class?
Can you provide compounds that target genes of the MAPK family in the treatment of cancer-related disease?
cypher: MATCH (d:Disease)<-[:TREATS_CtD]-(c:Compound)-[:BINDS_CbG]->(g:Gene) WHERE d.name CONTAINS 'cancer' AND g.name CONTAINS 'MAPK' RETURN c.name, g.name

question: Which distinct compounds whose name ends with 'sone' are known to cause the same side effects of 'Danazol'?
cypher: MATCH (c1:Compound {name: 'Danazol'})-[:CAUSES_CcSE]->(se:SideEffect)<-[:CAUSES_CcSE]-(c2:Compound) WHERE c2.name ENDS WITH 'sone' RETURN DISTINCT c2.name

question: Which compounds treat 'hypertension'?
List compounds that are used to treat 'rheumatoid arthritis'.
Which genes whose name starts with 'COX' are downregulated in the 'alcohol dependence' disease?
cypher: MATCH (d:Disease {name: 'alcohol dependence'})-[:DOWNREGULATES_DdG]->(g:Gene) WHERE g.name STARTS WITH 'COX' RETURN g.name

question: Can you list the actions associated with the binding of genes to the compound 'Azathioprine'?
What are the different actions associated with the binding of genes to compounds included in the pharmacologic class 'Adrenergic Agonists'?
Can you list the genes that participate in the biological process 'DNA repair' and that also partecipate in the 'phosphatase activity' molecular function?

Which compound-binds-gene triples are characterized by a non-null binding affinities? Return 5 triples order from highest affinity to lowest breaking ties alphabetically according to compound and gene name.
cypher: MATCH (c:Compound)-[r:BINDS_CbG]->(g:Gene) WHERE r.affinity_nM IS NOT NULL RETURN g.name, c.name, r.affinity_nM ORDER BY r.affinity_nM DESC, g.name ASC, c.name ASC LIMIT 5

question: With which genes does the gene 'BRCA1' interact and what pathways involving 'HIV' do they participate in?
cypher: MATCH (g1:Gene {name: 'BRCA1'})-[:INTERACTS_GiG]->(g2:Gene)-[:PARTICIPATES_GpPW]->(p:Pathway) WHERE p.name CONTAINS 'HIV' RETURN g2.name, p.name

question: What compounds are known to cause 'Cough' as side effect in patients treated for 'hypertension'?
What are the genes that are upregulated by compounds with a z-score above 100?
cypher: MATCH (c:Compound)-[r:UPREGULATES_CuG]->(g:Gene) WHERE r.z_score > 100 RETURN g.name, r.z_score

question: Which genes on chromosome '12' participate in the 'lysosome' cellular component?
With which genes does the gene 'BRCA1' interact, that participate in the molecular function named 'ubiquitin protein ligase binding' in which also 'BRCA1' participates.
cypher: MATCH (g1:Gene {name: 'BRCA1'})-[:INTERACTS_GiG]->(g2:Gene)-[:PARTICIPATES_GpMF]->(:MolecularFunction {name: 'ubiquitin protein ligase binding'})<-[:PARTICIPATES_GpMF]-(g1) RETURN g2.name

question: Which genes participating in the 'MAPK Signaling Pathway' interact with genes, when the interacting genes are on the same chromosome and that chromosome is '1'?
cypher: MATCH (g1:Gene)-[:INTERACTS_GiG]->(g2:Gene)-[:PARTICIPATES_GpPW]->(:Pathway {name: 'MAPK Signaling Pathway'}) WHERE g1.chromosome = g2.chromosome = '1' RETURN g1.name, g2.name

question: Can you find genes that interact with 'Dopamine' and are also associated with 'obesity'?
What are the actions associated with the binding of genes with the compound 'Metoprolol'?
List compounds that are known to palliate 'multiple sclerosis'.
What compounds are known to palliate 'breast cancer' but not 'lung cancer'?
cypher: MATCH (d:Disease {name: 'breast cancer'})<-[:PALLIATES_CpD]-(c:Compound) WHERE NOT (c)-[:PALLIATES_CpD]->(:Disease {name: 'lung cancer'}) RETURN c.name

question: List the genes that are bound by the compound 'Lisinopril' and their binding affinities.
Which compounds bind genes that the gene DACT3 covaries with?
List compounds that resemble each other in the graph, where both end with 'sone'.
cypher: MATCH (c1:Compound)-[:RESEMBLES_CrC]->(c2:Compound) WHERE c1.name ENDS WITH 'sone' AND c2.name ENDS WITH 'sone' RETURN c1.name, c2.name

question: Which genes covary with MAML2 and participate in the same pathway in which MAML2 participates?
What are the genes belonging to the PPP family that are upregulated by compounds in the pharmacologic class 'Vinca Alkaloids'?
cypher: MATCH (pc:PharmacologicClass {name: 'Vinca Alkaloids'})-[:INCLUDES_PCiC]->(c:Compound)-[:UPREGULATES_CuG]->(g:Gene) WHERE g.name CONTAINS 'PPP' RETURN g.name

question: Which genes interact with 'Ziprasidone' and participate in the molecular function 'aromatase activity'?
What compounds are commonly used for palliating 'Chronic Pain'?
What compounds are known to palliate 'atopic dermatitis' and bind to genes located on chromosome X?
cypher: MATCH (d:Disease {name: 'atopic dermatitis'})<-[:PALLIATES_CpD]-(c:Compound)-[:BINDS_CbG]->(g:Gene) WHERE g.chromosome = 'X' RETURN c.name, g.name

question: Can you provide compounds that cause the side effect 'Nausea' and whose name include the substrig 'xol'?
cypher: MATCH (se:SideEffect {name:'Nausea'})<-[:CAUSES_CcSE]-(c:Compound)  WHERE c.name CONTAINS 'xol' RETURN c.name

question: What genes are commonly expressed in the 'brain' and associated with 'schizophrenia'? Report only those whose name starts with 'SNAP'.
cypher: MATCH (a:Anatomy {name: 'brain'})-[:EXPRESSES_AeG]->(g:Gene)<-[:ASSOCIATES_DaG]-(d:Disease {name: 'schizophrenia'}) WHERE g.name STARTS WITH 'SNAP' RETURN g.name

question: Which diseases commonly present the symptom 'Speech Disorders'?
List the genes that regulate the gene 'TP53' and participate in the 'nuclear periphery' cellular component.
List the compounds that bind genes and are characterized by both the actions 'agonist' and 'partial agonist'.
cypher: MATCH (c:Compound)-[b:BINDS_CbG]->(g:Gene) WHERE 'agonist' IN b.actions AND 'partial agonist' IN b.actions RETURN c.name, g.name, b.actions

question: Which compounds treat 'primary biliary cirrhosis'?
List the compounds that treat 'atopic dermatitis', given that it is localized in 'sweat gland'
Which diseases commonly present the symptom 'Fatigue' and are localized in the 'vertebral column'?
What genes are commonly upregulated in 'schizophrenia' when the upregulation 'log2_fold_change' is stricly higher than 0.03?
cypher: MATCH (d:Disease {name: 'schizophrenia'})-[r:UPREGULATES_DuG]->(g:Gene) WHERE r.log2_fold_change > 0.03 RETURN g.name, r.log2_fold_change

question: Which genes participate in the 'DNA Repair' pathway and regulate genes where 'knockdown downregulates' and 'overexpression downregulates' are both subtypes of the regulation?
cypher: MATCH (g1:Gene)-[r:REGULATES_GrG]->(g2:Gene) WHERE (g1)-[:PARTICIPATES_GpPW]->(:Pathway {name: 'DNA Repair'}) AND 'overexpression downregulates' IN r.subtypes AND 'knockdown downregulates' IN r.subtypes RETURN g1.name, g2.name, r.subtypes

question: List the compounds that bind the gene 'TP53' and their binding actions.
What are the distinct lists of actions associated with the binding of the compound 'Metformin' to genes?
Can you provide genes that are upregulated in 'abdominal aortic aneurysm' with a log2 fold change above 0.2, ranked by log2 fold change in descending order?
cypher: MATCH (d:Disease {name:'abdominal aortic aneurysm'})-[r:UPREGULATES_DuG]->(g:Gene) WHERE r.log2_fold_change > 0.2 RETURN g.name, r.log2_fold_change ORDER BY r.log2_fold_change DESC

question: Can you provide the binding actions of genes that are bound by the compound 'Omeprazole'?
List the genes that have a binding relationship with the compound 'Metformin' and their associated actions.
List genes on chromosome '4' that participate in the cellular component in which the gene 'MYC' participates.
List the genes that participate in the molecular function 'nuclease activity' and their associated mRNA-related pathways.
cypher: MATCH (mf:MolecularFunction {name: 'nuclease activity'})<-[:PARTICIPATES_GpMF]-(g:Gene)-[:PARTICIPATES_GpPW]->(p:Pathway) WHERE p.name CONTAINS 'mRNA' RETURN g.name, p.name

question: Which compounds are known to palliate diseases that upregulate genes with a log2 fold change greater than 1, and what are those diseases and log2 fold changes?
cypher: MATCH (d:Disease)<-[:PALLIATES_CpD]-(c:Compound), (d)-[r:UPREGULATES_DuG]->(g:Gene) WHERE r.log2_fold_change > 1 RETURN c.name, d.name, r.log2_fold_change

question: Which genes interact with each other in the context of the disease 'gestational diabetes', when the sources of the interaction exceed 3 items and among those items 'hetio-dag' is listed?
cypher: MATCH (d:Disease {name: 'gestational diabetes'})-[:ASSOCIATES_DaG]->(g1:Gene)-[r:INTERACTS_GiG]->(g2:Gene) WHERE 'hetio-dag' IN r.sources AND SIZE(r.sources) > 3 RETURN g1.name, g2.name, r.sources

question: Can you identify the genes that participate in both the biological process 'DNA repair' and in the molecular function 'DNA-methyltransferase activity'?
For each diabete-related disease what is the list of symptoms that the disease presents?
cypher: MATCH (d:Disease)-[:PRESENTS_DpS]->(s:Symptom) WHERE d.name CONTAINS 'diabete' RETURN d.name, COLLECT(s.name)

question: Where does 'osteoporosis' localize within the body?
Find genes such that they resemble and interact with gene 'C1QBP'.
Which compounds are known to palliate 'hypertension' and are not known to cause the side effect 'Insomnia?
Investigate compounds that palliate the same cancer-related diseases and such that at least one of them includes the word 'Estrogens' in the name.
cypher: MATCH (c1:Compound)-[:PALLIATES_CpD]->(d1:Disease)<-[:PALLIATES_CpD]-(c2:Compound) WHERE c1 <> c2 AND d1.name CONTAINS 'cancer' AND c1.name CONTAINS 'Estrogens' OR c2.name CONTAINS 'Estrogens' RETURN c1.name, c2.name, d1.name

question: Which compounds whose name ends with 'ate' are known to cause either the side effect 'Insomnia' or 'Headache'?
cypher: MATCH (se:SideEffect)<-[:CAUSES_CcSE]-(c:Compound) WHERE se.name IN ['Insomnia', 'Headache'] AND c.name ENDS WITH 'ate' RETURN c.name

question: What diseases can be treated by compounds that bind the gene 'ALOX5'?
Which genes participate in the biological process named 'execution phase of apoptosis'.
cypher: MATCH (g:Gene)-[:PARTICIPATES_GpBP]->(:BiologicalProcess {name: 'execution phase of apoptosis'}) RETURN g.name

question: Which genes are associated with the disease 'bile duct cancer' with more than one evidence sources?
cypher: MATCH (d:Disease {name: 'bile duct cancer'})-[r:ASSOCIATES_DaG]->(g:Gene) WHERE SIZE(r.sources) > 1 RETURN g.name, r.sources

question: Retrieve the list of genes that participate in each apoptosis-related biological process?
cypher: MATCH (bp:BiologicalProcess )<-[:PARTICIPATES_GpBP]-(g:Gene) WHERE bp.name CONTAINS 'apoptosis' RETURN bp.name, COLLECT(g.name)

question: Identify the genes object of interaction with 'CIDEA' and that are associated with the same biological processes in ehich 'CIDEA' participates. Moreover, report the list of the associated shared biological processes.
Which specific compounds treat 'Asthma', and what are the list of skin-related side effect for each side effect?
cypher: MATCH (d:Disease {name: 'asthma'})<-[r:TREATS_CtD]-(c:Compound)-[:CAUSES_CcSE]->(se:SideEffect) WHERE se.name CONTAINS 'skin' RETURN c.name, COLLECT(se.name)

question: Identify the genes that regulate the gene NR1H2, when the regulation suptypes include the item 'overexpression upregulates'.
cypher: MATCH (g1:Gene {name: 'NR1H2'})<-[r:REGULATES_GrG]-(g2:Gene) WHERE 'overexpression upregulates' IN r.subtypes RETURN g2.name

question: What are the pain-related side effects caused by compounds that bind the gene 'EGFR'?
cypher: MATCH (g:Gene {name: 'EGFR'})<-[:BINDS_CbG]-(c:Compound)-[:CAUSES_CcSE]->(se:SideEffect) WHERE se.name CONTAINS 'pain' RETURN se.name

question: Can you provide the pubmed IDs of genes bound by compounds when those genes are regulated by the gene 'BRCA1'?
Find genes that: 1) BRCA1 covaries with; 2) participate in the 'telomere cap complex'.
Which genes interact with the gene 'PTEN' and participate in the same biological process with it, having more than 2 interaction sources including 'Lit-BM-13'?
cypher: MATCH (g1:Gene {name: 'PTEN'})-[r:INTERACTS_GiG]->(g2:Gene)-[:PARTICIPATES_GpBP]->(:BiologicalProcess)<-[:PARTICIPATES_GpBP]-(g1:Gene) WHERE 'Lit-BM-13' IN r.sources AND SIZE(r.sources) > 2 RETURN DISTINCT g2.name, r.sources

question: Which genes interact with the gene NR1H2 and participate in the same biological processes in which the mentioned gene participates, when interaction sources exceed 2 items (> 2) and one of those items is 'hetio-dag'?
cypher: MATCH (g1:Gene {name: 'NR1H2'})-[r:INTERACTS_GiG]->(g2:Gene)-[:PARTICIPATES_GpBP]->(:BiologicalProcess)<-[:PARTICIPATES_GpBP]-(g1:Gene) WHERE 'hetio-dag' IN r.sources AND SIZE(r.sources) > 2 RETURN DISTINCT g2.name, r.sources

question: Which genes participate in shared pathways with 'AKT1' and also share the same chromosome?
cypher: MATCH (g1:Gene {name: 'AKT1'})-[:PARTICIPATES_GpPW]->(:Pathway)<-[:PARTICIPATES_GpPW]-(g2:Gene) WHERE g2.chromosome = g1.chromosome RETURN g2.name, g2.chromosome

question: Which compounds whose name includes the word 'acid' cause side effects similar to those caused by 'Metformin'?
cypher: MATCH (c:Compound {name: 'Metformin'})-[:CAUSES_CcSE]->(se:SideEffect)<-[:CAUSES_CcSE]-(c2:Compound) WHERE c2.name CONTAINS 'acid' RETURN c2.name

question: Can you find compounds that downregulate genes downregulated also by 'Metformin', having a 'z_score' for the dowregulation with 'Metformin' below -6 (z_score < -6)?
cypher: MATCH (c1:Compound {name: 'Metformin'})-[r:DOWNREGULATES_CdG]->(g:Gene)<-[:DOWNREGULATES_CdG]-(c2:Compound) WHERE r.z_score < -6 RETURN c2.name, r.z_score

question: Which genes are associated with the progression of 'bile duct cancer', when the asociation is characterized by more than one source?
cypher: MATCH (d:Disease {name: 'bile duct cancer'})-[r:ASSOCIATES_DaG]->(g:Gene) WHERE SIZE(r.sources) > 1 RETURN g.name, r.sources

question: Which compounds bind genes and treat cancer-related diseases, when the binding 'affinity_nM' is below 0.5?
cypher: MATCH (d:Disease)<-[:TREATS_CtD]-(c:Compound)-[r:BINDS_CbG]->(g:Gene) WHERE d.name CONTAINS 'cancer' AND r.affinity_nM < 0.5 RETURN c.name, g.name, d.name, r.affinity_nM

question: What diseases have a resemblance to 'alcohol dependence' based on their clinical presentation?
Which diseases have a resemblance to 'hepatitis B' and what are the sources of resemblance?
Which compounds bind genes and treat 'bone cancer', when the binding is characterized by non-null 'affinity_nM'?
cypher: MATCH (d:Disease {name: 'bone cancer'})<-[:TREATS_CtD]-(c:Compound)-[r:BINDS_CbG]->(g:Gene) WHERE r.affinity_nM IS NOT NULL RETURN c.name, g.name, d.name, r.affinity_nM

question: Which compounds bind genes and treat liver-related diseases, when the binding 'affinity_nM' is not null?
cypher: MATCH (d:Disease)<-[:TREATS_CtD]-(c:Compound)-[r:BINDS_CbG]->(g:Gene) WHERE d.name CONTAINS 'liver' AND r.affinity_nM IS NOT NULL RETURN c.name, g.name, d.name, r.affinity_nM

question: Which compounds whose name ends with 'sone' are known to cause either the side effect 'Aggression' or 'Feeling abnormal' ?
cypher: MATCH (se:SideEffect)<-[:CAUSES_CcSE]-(c:Compound) WHERE se.name IN ['Aggression', 'Feeling abnormal'] AND c.name ENDS WITH 'sone' RETURN c.name

question: List the genes that are characterized by non-null binding affinities with the compound 'Lisinopril'.
cypher: MATCH (g:Gene)-[r:BINDS_CbG]-(c:Compound {name: 'Lisinopril'}) WHERE r.affinity_nM IS NOT NULL RETURN g.name, r.affinity_nM

question: List the genes that are characterized by high binding affinities (let's say 100) with a compound.
Provide the chromosome of genes that interact with the compound 'Ruxolitinib' and are characterized by a binding 'affinity_nM' above 2.
cypher: MATCH (c:Compound {name: 'Ruxolitinib'})-[r:BINDS_CbG]->(g:Gene) WHERE r.affinity_nM > 2 RETURN g.name, g.chromosome, r.affinity_nM

question: List diseases that commonly present 'Dizziness'. For each disease, specify its list of anatomical localizations.
Which diseases commonly present 'Bulimia' as symptom?
Which genes are downregulated in 'hypertension'?
Which diseases localize in the 'immune system'?
Which compounds treat or palliate 'rheumatoid arthritis'? Include the type of relationship in the output.
What are the compounds that palliate both 'endogenous depression' and 'multiple sclerosis'?
Can you provide a list of compounds that bind with genes belonging to the PDE family such that binding interactions are associated with 'inhibitor' actions?
cypher: MATCH (c:Compound)-[r:BINDS_CbG]->(g:Gene) WHERE 'inhibitor' IN r.actions AND g.name CONTAINS 'PDE' RETURN c.name, g.name, r.actions

question: Which compounds have a binding relationship with the gene 'TP53'?
Which compounds have a similar effect to the compound 'Azelastine'?
Which compounds treat or palliate in patients with 'systemic scleroderma'? Include the type of relationship in the output.
Which 5 compound-binds-gene interactions have the highest non-null binding affinities? To avoid ties, order them alphabetically by compound name and gene name.
cypher: MATCH (c:Compound)-[r:BINDS_CbG]->(g:Gene) WHERE r.affinity_nM IS NOT NULL RETURN c.name, g.name, r.affinity_nM ORDER BY r.affinity_nM DESC, c.name ASC, g.name ASC LIMIT 5

question: Which 5 compound-binds-gene interactions have the lowest non-null binding affinities? To avoid ties, order them alphabetically by compound name and gene name.
cypher: MATCH (c:Compound)-[r:BINDS_CbG]->(g:Gene) WHERE r.affinity_nM IS NOT NULL RETURN c.name, g.name, r.affinity_nM ORDER BY r.affinity_nM ASC, c.name ASC, g.name ASC LIMIT 5

question: List the genes that are downregulated by compounds in the context of treating 'osteoporosis', when the downregulation 'z_score' is low (let's say < -10).
cypher: MATCH (d:Disease {name: 'osteoporosis'})<-[:TREATS_CtD]-(c:Compound)-[r:DOWNREGULATES_CdG]->(g:Gene) WHERE r.z_score < -10 RETURN g.name, r.z_score

question: Which are the top 5 genes with the highest downregulation 'z_score' in response to compounds that treat osteoporosis, considering only 'z_score' > -5? In case of ties, order alphabetically by gene name.
cypher: MATCH (:Disease {name: 'osteoporosis'})<-[:TREATS_CtD]-(c:Compound)-[r:DOWNREGULATES_CdG]->(g:Gene) WHERE r.z_score > -5 WITH g, MAX(r.z_score) AS max_z_score RETURN g.name, max_z_score ORDER BY max_z_score DESC, g.name ASC LIMIT 5

question: Which are the top 5 genes with the lowest downregulation 'z_score' in response to compounds that treat 'systemic scleroderma', considering only 'z_score' > -5? In case of ties, order alphabetically by gene name.
cypher: MATCH (:Disease {name: 'systemic scleroderma'})<-[:TREATS_CtD]-(c:Compound)-[r:DOWNREGULATES_CdG]->(g:Gene) WHERE r.z_score > -5 WITH g, MIN(r.z_score) AS min_z_score RETURN g.name, min_z_score ORDER BY min_z_score ASC, g.name ASC LIMIT 5

question: List the genes that regulate the gene 'EGFR' and for each gene propose the list of the associated molecular functions in which it participates.
cypher: MATCH (g1:Gene {name: 'EGFR'})<-[:REGULATES_GrG]-(g2:Gene)-[:PARTICIPATES_GpMF]->(mf:MolecularFunction) RETURN g2.name, COLLECT(DISTINCT mf.name) AS molecular_functions

question: Which genes have the maximum binding affinities with compounds and what are the affinities? Consider only high affinities (> 900).
cypher: MATCH (c:Compound)-[b:BINDS_CbG]->(g:Gene) WHERE b.affinity_nM > 900 RETURN g.name, MAX(b.affinity_nM) AS max_affinity

question: Which genes have the minimum binding affinities with compounds and what are the affinities? Consider only low affinities (< 0.3).
cypher: MATCH (c:Compound)-[b:BINDS_CbG]->(g:Gene) WHERE b.affinity_nM < 0.3  RETURN g.name, MIN(b.affinity_nM) AS min_affinity

question: Which genes co-vary with each other and participate in the same cellular component named 'mitotic spindle'?
What genes are associated with 'asthma' and 'hepatitis B'?
Identify compounds that palliate diseases also treated by 'Metformin'.
Whic compounds bind genes and treat 'pancreatitis'?
What are the compounds that bind the gene 'GABRA3' such that the 'pubmed_ids' for the association include '10209232'?
cypher: MATCH (g:Gene {name: 'GABRA3'})<-[r:BINDS_CbG]-(c:Compound) WHERE '10209232' IN r.pubmed_ids RETURN c.name, r.pubmed_ids

question: Which genes interact with 'GNA12' and participate in the same pathway in which 'GNA12' participates?
Which genes are characterized by binding relationships with the compound 'Metoprolol' when the latter is involved in downregulation?
What are the molecular functions participated by genes that participate in the cellular component named 'proteasome accessory complex'?
Return distinct genes that bind compounds included in the pharmacologic class 'Penicillins'.
Find genes that interact with 'PTEN' and participate in the same biological process named 'heart development' in which also 'PTEN' participates.
Which genes are downregulated by compounds that resemble 'Ziprasidone'?
Which compounds are used for both treating and palliating cancer-related diseases?
cypher: MATCH (d1:Disease )<-[:TREATS_CtD]-(c:Compound)-[:PALLIATES_CpD]->(d2:Disease) WHERE d1.name CONTAINS 'cancer' AND d2.name CONTAINS 'cancer' RETURN c.name, d1.name, d2.name

question: Which genes interact with gene 'AKT1' and participate in the same pathway named 'Estrogen signaling pathway' in which also 'AKT1' participate?
Find genes that are downregulated in 'midbrain' and interact with the gene 'HNF4A'.
Can you provide a list of compounds that have multiple binding interactions (more than 30) with genes?
cypher: MATCH (c:Compound)-[:BINDS_CbG]->(g:Gene) WITH c, COUNT(g) AS num_genes WHERE num_genes > 30 RETURN c.name, num_genes

question: Can you provide all the compounds whose name ends with the substring 'sone'?
cypher: MATCH (c:Compound) WHERE c.name ends with 'sone' RETURN c.name

question: Can you provide all the nerve-related Anatomy?
cypher: MATCH (a:Anatomy) WHERE a.name CONTAINS 'nerve' RETURN a.name

question: Can you provide all gene on chromosome 'Y' that are described as 'Y-linked'?
cypher: MATCH (g:Gene) WHERE g.chromosome = 'Y' AND g.description CONTAINS 'Y-linked' RETURN g.name, g.description

question: What are the distinct class types that characterize pharmacologic classes?
Provide all the sleep-related symptoms.
cypher: MATCH (s:Symptom) WHERE toLower(s.name) CONTAINS 'sleep' RETURN s.name

question: Provide all the pain-related side effects.
cypher: MATCH (se:SideEffect) WHERE toLower(se.name) CONTAINS 'pain' RETURN se.name
